title: celtis planchoniana
intro: Celtis planchoniana, also known as the Net-Barked Hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive net-like patterns on its bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Celtis planchoniana include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating coughs, as well as providing antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
